meta.description: Generic overlay: maps each component_catalog id in decision-tree.json to one or more generic UI component names. Entries with gap:true have no confirmed component in a typical design system and should be reviewed before implementation. Update the 'component' field with your design system's actual component names when adopting this library.
meta.governance: A gap:true resolution requires design system review before build — never an ad-hoc custom build without confirmation.

mappings.notes: Standard single-line text entry. Use type attribute for structured values (email, tel, url).
Multi-line text entry for longer authored responses.
Search form with labelled input and submit. May include result count announcement.
Use text-input with inputmode=numeric and appropriate validation.
Range slider. Verify your design system has a slider component before use.
Grouped date fields for memorable dates. Verify your design system has a date-input component.
Calendar-assisted date selection.
Paired start/end date inputs with calendar. May compose from two date-picker instances.
Free time entry. Verify your design system has a time-input component.
Selection from finite appointment/session times as radio buttons or selectable tiles. May compose from radio-group.
Standard file upload with accepted formats, size, and multi-file states.
Single independent opt-in or acknowledgment within a form.
Immediate on/off binary setting.
fieldset + legend + radio inputs for mutually exclusive single choice.
Rich/descriptive/visual single-choice tiles. Verify your design system has radio-cards.
Small mutually exclusive view/mode switch. May compose from radio inputs or button-group.
Compact single-choice list.
Searchable/typeahead single selection.
Search-first selection from very large/dynamic/remote corpus. Verify your design system has an autocomplete component.
fieldset + legend + checkbox inputs for visible multi-selection.
Search, add, and remove multiple items from a large known set. Verify your design system has a multi-select component.
Multiple collection constraints exposed together.
Visible summary and removal of active filters.
Ordering control. May compose from a select component.
Search with labelled filter refinements.
Ordered/unordered list of repeated items. Use semantic ul/ol.
Rich distinct objects with preview metadata.
Static data table with caption and headers.
Interactive/editable tabular workspace requiring ARIA grid pattern. Verify your design system has a data-grid component.
Hierarchical treeview. Verify your design system has a tree component.
Hierarchical interactive table. Verify your design system has a treegrid component.
Accessible visualization plus data summary/table. Verify your design system has chart components.
Concise categorical state label (not color alone).
Known-progress completion indicator.
Non-interactive layout placeholders while content loads.
Indeterminate short-wait indicator with accessible status announcement.
Heading, explanation, and relevant action for empty collections.
Field-specific correction guidance adjacent to field.
Form-wide error summary with focus on submit failure.
Persistent page or section status (warning, info, error). role=status or alert.
Transient low-risk confirmation. Non-blocking with accessible announcement.
Blocking decision or focused required task. ARIA dialog pattern.
Contextual support without blocking main task. Side panel / drawer pattern.
Button controlling expandable region for supplemental content.
Multiple independently collapsible optional sections.
ARIA tabs pattern for peer panels within the same context.
Required ordered stages with step indicator.
Standard command or state-change trigger.
Use button with the primary variant/modifier.
Button revealing a compact list of related actions.
Infrequent item actions without visual clutter. Menu button pattern.
Recurring controls for a workspace or selection. Verify your design system has a toolbar component.
Primary command with closely related alternate modes. Verify your design system has a split-button component.
Navigation to another destination or resource. Semantic <a href>.
Primary product destination navigation (nav with links).
Multi-page section/hierarchy navigation with current page indication.
Ancestor path and location context (nav aria-label Breadcrumb with ordered links).
Fragment links to sections on a long page.
Navigation through addressable pages of results.
Append-results button pattern. Compose from a button — no standalone component required.
Continuous feed consumption. Feed ARIA pattern only. Implement cautiously with navigation alternatives.
Brief supplementary message on focus or hover. Accessible tooltip pattern.

summary.note: gap:true entries (component:null) require design system review before build. Update this file with your own design system's component names.
